arguments.task: Visit official Monday.com sources to verify: 1) one-line description of monday.com developer platform/API, 2) API auth methods and auth docs, 3) pricing or access requirements for API/developer access, 4) API surface types (REST and/or GraphQL), 5) rate limits, 6) whether monday.com has an official MCP server or official MCP documentation, 7) any official changelog or support docs relevant to buildability. Prefer first-party docs pages and extract exact URLs used as evidence. Return concise findings with source URLs.

input.thought: Run a focused browser research task on official Monday.com documentation and related first-party pages.

Our API is an application layer that allows apps to read and update data inside a monday.com account.\\\\\\\"\\n*   **Source:** [https://developer.monday.com/api-reference/docs/basics](https://developer.monday.com/api-reference/docs/basics)\\n\\n**2) API Auth Methods and Docs**\\n*   **Methods:** Primarily uses **Personal V2 API tokens** (passed in `Authorization` header). raw_result.description: Work OS with a GraphQL API that lets apps read and update data inside a monday.com account.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official auth docs describe personal V2 API tokens sent in the Authorization header and app/OAuth-based access for apps and guests; evidence also mentions shortLivedToken for some guest/app contexts, so token details beyond bearer-style Authorization may need confirmation.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public developer docs are available and API access exists on self-serve plans, with usage limits varying by plan tier; no contact-sales or partner gate was evidenced for initial developer access.

api_surface.breadth: Public GraphQL API for reading and updating monday.com account data, with authentication, rate limits, playground, release notes, and API coverage gaps documented.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Platform MCP for monday.com is documented as available, including open-source and hosted options.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs cite tier-based limits including complexity budgets (5M points/min for standard accounts and 1M for trial/free), daily call caps (1,000 free/basic/standard, 10,000 pro, 25,000 enterprise), plus minute/concurrency limits and RateLimit-Policy / RateLimit headers.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official MCP documentation exists; official release notes are maintained; docs explicitly note API coverage gaps; auth/access varies by user type (admins/members vs guests/viewers/student accounts).
raw_result.human_review_reason: Evidence gathered via browser extraction summarized official pages, but some access-tier and auth nuances are ambiguous: the extracted notes conflict slightly on whether guests can access via OAuth/shortLivedToken and whether Free/Basic/Standard daily-call grouping is exact. Human review should confirm plan-tier mapping and the precise supported auth modes exposed to third-party tool builders.
